color_harmony_bank_v1.description: Modular color harmony and palette observation fragments. Designed for short, conversational styling feedback that feels human, supportive, and practical.

positive_harmony: The colors work beautifully together.
The palette feels cohesive.
The tones complement each other nicely.
This color combination feels intentional.
There’s a smooth flow between the shades.
The contrast is balanced.
The color pairing feels elevated.
These tones sit well next to each other.
The palette looks clean and refined.
The combination feels effortless.
The shades blend in a really pleasing way.
This palette feels thoughtfully chosen.
The contrast level feels just right.
The tones create a calm, balanced look.
The color mix feels confident and put together.
This palette feels expensive and well coordinated.
The colors feel intentional, not accidental.
The tones are blending really smoothly.
This is a clean, confident color story.
The palette feels balanced—nothing is screaming.
This color mix photographs really well.
The contrast level feels stylish, not harsh.
The colors feel modern and put together.
This pairing looks polished and easy.
The shades feel like they belong together.
positive_skin_tone_alignment: This color enhances your undertone.
That shade really brightens your complexion.
The tone adds warmth in a good way.
This color makes the whole look pop subtly.
The shade complements your natural coloring.
This tone brings out your natural glow.
The color adds depth without overpowering you.
This shade looks really fresh against your skin.
The warmth in this color works beautifully on you.
The tone feels harmonious with your overall coloring.
This shade makes your skin look brighter and fresher.
This tone brings out warmth in a really flattering way.
This color sits beautifully against your natural coloring.
This shade adds a nice glow effect without overpowering.
The undertone match here is really working.
This tone makes the whole look feel more alive.
This color lifts your overall vibe in a subtle way.
This shade gives a really clean, flattering contrast.
This tone feels very you on a skin level.
This color looks naturally harmonious on you.
constructive_flat_or_clashing: The combination feels slightly flat.
There isn’t much contrast between the pieces.
The tones are competing a little.
The palette feels slightly disconnected.
The shades don’t fully harmonize.
It could use a stronger anchor color.
The colors are close in tone but not quite aligned.
The combination feels a bit muted.
There’s too much similarity between the pieces.
The contrast could be sharper.
The palette feels a little unstructured right now.
The tones are leaning in different directions.
The contrast is either too soft or slightly uneven.
The colors are individually nice but not fully cohesive together.
It needs a clearer color hierarchy.
The palette is fine, but it needs one clearer anchor color.
The tones are close, so the outfit reads a bit blended.
The colors feel slightly unbalanced in intensity.
One shade feels like it’s pulling away from the rest.
It’s not clashing, but it’s not fully clicking yet.
The palette feels a little undecided right now.
The contrast is either a bit too soft or uneven.
It needs a cleaner base plus accent structure.
The colors feel nice individually, but not unified together.
One tone is slightly dulling the overall look.
refinement_suggestions: Adding one deeper tone would ground it.
Introducing a contrasting accessory could balance it.
A lighter layer would break this up nicely.
Switching one piece to a neutral could refine it.
Adding one bold accent would make it pop.
A cleaner color contrast would elevate it.
Reducing one competing shade would simplify it.
A warmer tone could soften the look.
A cooler shade might balance this better.
One statement color would anchor everything.
Keeping the palette to two or three tones would make it feel more intentional.
Adding a neutral base would make the other colors stand out more.
Swapping one piece to a slightly richer shade would elevate the whole look.
Choosing one dominant color and supporting it with softer tones would help.
A subtle contrast in accessories would add just enough interest.
If you add one neutral base (black, cream, or navy), the palette will look more intentional.
If you repeat one color in your shoes or bag, it’ll tie together instantly.
If you swap one piece to a richer shade, it’ll look more premium.
If you reduce one competing tone, everything will feel cleaner.
A slightly deeper accent would ground the look nicely.
A lighter layer would add contrast without changing the whole outfit.
A cleaner warm-or-cool direction would make it more cohesive.
One statement color—just one—would give the palette a focal point.
If you keep it to two or three tones, it’ll feel elevated immediately.
A tonal accessory in the same family would make it look expensive.

usage_rules.default_pattern: Use 1 color observation. Add 1 refinement only if the user requests improvement or expresses dissatisfaction.
avoid: Stacking multiple constructive color critiques in one short reply.
Using technical color theory language unless the user asks for depth.
Making assumptions about skin tone beyond visible harmony comments.
usage_rules.tone_integration: Fragments must follow AHVI response structure (Reaction → Observation → Optional refinement → Invitation). Keep it conversational and supportive.
